title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: A story of wealth, class, and the elusive American Dream set in the Roaring Twenties, following the mysterious millionaire Jay Gatsby and his obsession with the beautiful Daisy Buchanan

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: Seen through the eyes of young Scout Finch, this Pulitzer Prize-winning novel explores racial injustice and moral growth in Depression-era Alabama as her father Atticus defends a Black man falsely accused of a crime

author: George Orwell
description: A chilling portrait of a totalitarian society where Big Brother watches every move, Winston Smith secretly rebels against a government that controls history, language, and thought itself

title: Dune
author: Frank Herbert
description: An epic saga set on the desert planet Arrakis, where young Paul Atreides navigates treacherous political intrigue, ecological mysticism, and his destiny as a prophesied leader among the Fremen people

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: Reluctant hobbit Bilbo Baggins is swept into an unexpected adventure with thirteen dwarves and the wizard Gandalf to reclaim the dwarves' mountain kingdom from the fearsome dragon Smaug

title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: A witty and perceptive novel about Elizabeth Bennet navigating love, pride, and social expectations in Regency England as she clashes with the aloof and wealthy Mr. Darcy

title: Brave New World
author: Aldous Huxley
description: In a future World State engineered for stability and happiness, Bernard Marx questions a society built on genetic predetermination, conditioning, and soma — a drug that keeps citizens blissfully compliant

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A young Andalusian shepherd named Santiago journeys from Spain to the Egyptian desert in search of buried treasure and discovers that the real treasure lies within, guided by omens and the Soul of the World

title: Sapiens
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A sweeping intellectual history of humankind from the Stone Age through the twenty-first century, exploring how Homo sapiens came to dominate the planet through cognitive revolution, agriculture, and shared myths

title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
author: Douglas Adams
description: Hapless Arthur Dent is whisked off Earth seconds before it is demolished to make way for a hyperspace bypass, beginning a wildly absurdist journey through the universe with his alien friend Ford Prefect
